title: What is the grace period for Star Health Family Health Optima?
content: A grace period of 30 days is available from the date of expiration of the policy for renewal. During the grace period, coverage is not available. However, the continuity benefit is maintained if the policy is renewed within the grace period.
source_document_title: Star Health FAQ — Family Health Optima

title: What is the waiting period for pre-existing diseases under Family Health Optima?
content: Pre-existing diseases have a waiting period of 36 months from the date of first inception of the policy. Specific diseases and procedures have a waiting period of 24 months. The initial waiting period for any illness is 30 days from policy start, except for accidents which are covered from day one.
source_document_title: Star Health FAQ — Family Health Optima

title: Who can be covered under Family Health Optima?
content: The Family Health Optima Insurance Plan covers the entire family under a single sum insured on a floater basis. Eligible members include self, spouse, dependent children from 16 days up to 25 years, parents, and parents-in-law. The primary insured must be between 18 and 65 years of age at entry.
source_document_title: Star Health FAQ — Family Health Optima

title: Can I pay the premium in instalments?
content: Yes. Premium can be paid monthly, quarterly, or half-yearly. For two-year term policies with instalment facility, the full premium for the first year must be paid within the first year itself in monthly, quarterly, or half-yearly instalments.
source_document_title: Star Health FAQ — Family Health Optima

title: What is the loyalty bonus under Family Health Optima?
content: For sum insured options of Rs. 3 lakh and above, the insured is entitled to a loyalty bonus of 10% of the expiring sum insured per year, subject to a maximum accumulation of 100% of the sum insured. The loyalty bonus is available irrespective of claims and only upon timely renewal without a break or within the grace period.
source_document_title: Star Health FAQ — Family Health Optima

title: Does Family Health Optima cover newborn babies?
content: Yes. Hospitalisation expenses for newborn babies are covered from the 16th day after birth. Coverage is subject to a limit of 10% of the sum insured or Rs. 50,000, whichever is less. The mother must be insured under the policy for a continuous period of 12 months without a break for this benefit to apply.
source_document_title: Star Health FAQ — Family Health Optima

title: What is the free look period for Family Health Optima?
content: A free look period of 30 days is available from the date of receipt of the policy document for new policies only. It does not apply to renewals or portability. If the policyholder is not satisfied and has not made any claim, they may cancel the policy and receive a refund of premium after deduction of proportionate risk premium and stamp duty charges.
source_document_title: Star Health FAQ — Family Health Optima

title: How does the automatic restoration of sum insured work?
content: Upon exhaustion of the sum insured during the policy period, 100% of the sum insured is automatically restored up to 3 times in the same policy year. The restored sum insured can only be used for illnesses or diseases different from those for which earlier claims were made. This benefit does not apply to modern treatments. Each restoration activates only after the previous one is exhausted.
source_document_title: Star Health FAQ — Family Health Optima

title: How to file a cashless claim with Star Health
content: For cashless claims, contact the insurance desk at the network hospital and share your Customer ID and policy copy. For planned hospitalisation, intimate Star Health 7 to 10 days in advance. For emergency hospitalisation, notify within 24 hours of admission. The hospital submits a pre-authorisation form to Star Health. The claims team reviews and responds within 2 hours. Once approved, treatment proceeds on a cashless basis. Pay any difference not covered by the policy before discharge.

title: How to file a reimbursement claim with Star Health
content: For reimbursement claims at non-network hospitals, pay the hospital expenses directly. Within 15 days of discharge, submit all original documents to Star Health including: duly filled claim form, discharge summary, pre-admission investigation and treatment papers, final bill with detailed breakup, cash receipts from hospital and chemist, receipts from attending doctors and surgeons, and a copy of PAN card, Aadhaar card, and cancelled cheque or NEFT details. Star Health verifies documents and processes reimbursement within 7 days of approval.

title: Star Health claim settlement performance
content: Star Health settles 90% of cashless claims within 2 hours and 92% of reimbursement claims within 7 days. Star Health is India's first standalone health insurance company to settle claims in-house without any Third Party Administrator, using a qualified internal team of doctors. For claim assistance, call 1800-425-2255 (toll-free) or WhatsApp on +91-9597652225.

title: Family Health Optima — entry age and eligibility
content: The primary insured must be between 18 and 65 years of age at entry. Dependent children are covered from 16 days up to 25 years of age. Parents and parents-in-law can also be covered under the floater. For insured persons aged 61 years and above at entry, a mandatory co-payment of 20% applies to each and every claim. Pre-insurance medical screening is not required up to age 45 for sum insured up to Rs. 15 lakh.

title: Family Health Optima — sum insured options
content: The Family Health Optima Insurance Plan is available with sum insured options ranging from Rs. 3 lakh to Rs. 25 lakh. The policy can be taken for a term of 1 year or 2 years. Premium payment options include annual, half-yearly, quarterly, and monthly instalments. Lifelong renewals are guaranteed beyond 65 years of age.

title: Family Health Optima — pre-existing disease declaration
content: All pre-existing diseases must be declared at the time of proposal. Failure to disclose pre-existing conditions may result in claim rejection. Pre-existing diseases are covered after a waiting period of 36 months of continuous coverage. The insurer may apply loadings or exclusions based on the medical history disclosed. Health declarations are reviewed by Star Health's underwriting team before policy issuance.

title: Objection: The premium is too expensive
content: I understand your concern about the premium. Let's look at it differently — the Family Health Optima premium works out to approximately Rs. 50 to Rs. 150 per day for the entire family depending on your sum insured and age. That covers hospitalisation, day care procedures, pre and post-hospitalisation expenses, and even ambulance charges. A single hospitalisation can easily cost Rs. 50,000 or more. The plan also has a loyalty bonus that grows every year, so your effective coverage increases without additional premium.

title: Objection: I already have coverage from my employer
content: That's great that your employer provides group coverage. However, employer group policies typically end when you change jobs or retire, leaving you uninsured at exactly the time you may need it most. The Family Health Optima is a personal policy that stays with you regardless of your employment status. It also covers your parents and parents-in-law, who are often excluded from employer group policies. Starting now also means the 36-month waiting period for pre-existing diseases begins today.

title: Objection: I will decide later
content: I completely understand — this is an important decision. However, health insurance premiums increase with age, so every year of delay means higher premiums for the same coverage. More importantly, the 36-month waiting period for pre-existing diseases starts only when you take the policy. Any health condition that develops before you enroll will be treated as a pre-existing disease. Starting today gives you the earliest possible coverage and locks in a lower premium based on your current age.

title: Objection: I am healthy and do not need insurance
content: That is actually the best time to get insured. Insurance companies prefer healthy applicants and offer better terms with no loadings or exclusions. Once a health condition develops, coverage for that condition may be excluded or attract higher premiums. The Family Health Optima also rewards you for staying healthy through its Star Wellness Program, where you can earn up to 20% discount on renewal premium by completing wellness activities.

title: Objection: I do not trust insurance companies for claim settlement
content: Star Health has one of the strongest claim settlement records in India. We settle 90% of cashless claims within 2 hours and 92% of reimbursement claims within 7 days. We are India's first standalone health insurance company and settle all claims in-house without any Third Party Administrator. Our network of 14,000 plus hospitals across India ensures you can access cashless treatment almost anywhere. You can also track your claim status in real time through our app.

title: Objection: The waiting period is too long
content: The 36-month waiting period applies only to pre-existing diseases — conditions you already have before taking the policy. All new illnesses and accidents are covered from day 30 and day 1 respectively. Specific diseases have a shorter 24-month waiting period. The sooner you start, the sooner the waiting period is behind you. Many customers who delayed purchasing and then developed a condition wish they had started the waiting period clock earlier.

title: IRDAI Mandatory Insurance Disclosure
content: This is an insurance product regulated by the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India. IRDAI UIN: SHAHLIP26046V092526. Please read the policy terms and conditions carefully before purchasing. Premiums are subject to revision at renewal with prior approval from IRDAI. Past performance is not indicative of future benefits. Health insurance coverage for pre-existing medical conditions is subject to underwriting review and may involve additional requirements, loadings, or exclusions. Please disclose your complete medical history in the proposal form.

title: Star Health — Do Not Disturb and Consent Disclosure
content: By providing your details, you consent to receive assistance from Star Health regarding purchases and services through any valid communication channel including phone, SMS, email, and WhatsApp. If you wish to opt out of marketing communications, you may register on the TRAI DND registry. This call may be recorded for quality and training purposes.
